headline: SanDisk Vs. Micron: Why One of These Memory Stocks is Much More Dangerous Than the Other
summary: Both SanDisk and Micron just posted blockbuster AI-driven quarters, but one of these memory stocks carries a structural flaw that a brutal selloff just exposed in dramatic fashion.

raw.headline: SanDisk Vs. Micron: Why One of These Memory Stocks is Much More Dangerous Than the Other

headline: Seagate Stock Rises After Analyst Upgrades To Buy. Western Digital Nabs Price-Target Hike.
summary: Seagate Technology stock rose Friday, bouncing back from a recent pullback.  "With the recent market pullback and increasing confidence in what we view as a path to plus-$50 earnings-per-share and significant (capital) return capacity ahead, we upgrade STX to overweight with a $1,100 price target," Wells Fargo analyst Aaron Rakers said in a note for clients.  Rakers previously held a neutral equal-weight view for Seagate and a 900 price target.

headline: Western Digital (WDC) Stock Gets A Fresh AI Boost From HAMR And New Deals
summary: Western Digital (NasdaqGS:WDC) is benefiting from a sector-wide rally in AI and memory chip stocks, supported by easing geopolitical tensions. Fresh attention on AI infrastructure follows SK Hynix’s high-profile U.S. IPO and Micron’s large U.S. manufacturing expansion. Western Digital is pushing ahead with high-capacity HAMR drive technology and has secured multi-year commercial agreements running through 2028. Western Digital is back in the spotlight as investors refocus on the storage...

headline: Cloud Giants Record $1.1T RPO: A Growth Catalyst for Seagate?
summary: STX sees record cloud commitments and rising AI infrastructure spending fueling demand for its HAMR-based storage, with growth targets climbing higher.

headline: Western Digital Corp (NASDAQ:WDC) Shows Perfect Technical Rating and Strong High-Growth Momentum
summary: A systematic screen combining Mark Minervini’s Trend Template with high-growth momentum identifies Western Digital (WDC) as a strong candidate. WDC exhibits a confirmed Stage 2 uptrend with elite relative strength, accelerating earnings and revenue growth, and expanding profit margins.

headline: Micron's $250 Billion Bet Powers a Chip Comeback, But Pepsi and Levi Get Punished Anyway
summary: Chip stocks led Wall Street higher Thursday after Micron raised its US manufacturing pledge to $250 billion and a leaked Meta memo lit a fire under optics names, while PepsiCo and Levi Strauss both fell despite solid quarters. Oil retreated from its Iran-driven spike, existing home sales cooled again, and Meta put a price tag on its best AI model for the first time.
